title: Establish docs/ and manual/ directories in qms-cli

comment:
PRE-REVIEW: CR-103 v0.1

REVIEW CHECKLIST:

1. Frontmatter title: PASS. Value: "Establish docs/ and manual/ directories in qms-cli"
2. Frontmatter revision_summary: PASS. Value: "Initial draft" (appropriate for v0.x)
3. Document structure: PASS. All 12 required CR sections present per template.
4. Placeholder content: PASS. No {{...}}, TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found. Execution placeholders [...] are appropriately in Sections 10-11 only.
5. Traceability: PASS. INV-013 (CLOSED), CR-102 (CLOSED), SDLC-QMS-RS (EFFECTIVE v19.0), SDLC-QMS-RTM (EFFECTIVE v24.0) all confirmed. Commit 6e73be8 in Section 7.5 matches current qms-cli HEAD.
6. Internal consistency - EI/plan alignment: PASS. 12 EIs map 1:1 to 10 implementation phases plus pre/post execution baselines.
7. VR flags: PASS. Only EI-9 (integration verification) flagged, appropriate for behavioral verification of qms init.
8. Scope clarity: PASS. Explicit deferral of pipe-dream/QMS-Docs/ migration to follow-up CR.
9. Impact assessment: PASS. Files, documents, existing projects, new projects, and development controls all addressed.
10. Formatting consistency: PASS. Headings, rules, bold, code, and tables are consistent throughout.

DEFICIENCY (1):

Inconsistent file count for docs/ directory. Section 2.2 item 1 states "(6 files)" and Section 5.1 states "Six new files." However, Section 2.3 enumerates exactly 7 files: README.md, getting-started.md, cli-reference.md, project-structure.md, configuration.md, users-and-permissions.md, mcp-server.md. Sections 4, 7.1, and 9.3 all correctly state "7 files." The count "6" in Sections 2.2 and 5.1 is factually incorrect and must be corrected to "7."

DECISION: REQUEST-UPDATES. One factual error found. Correct the file count in Section 2.2 item 1 (change "6 files" to "7 files") and Section 5.1 (change "Six new files" to "Seven new files"), then re-route for review.

NOTE ON TU ASSIGNMENT: No Technical Unit reviewers were assigned. This CR modifies qms-cli infrastructure (init command, tests, documentation structure) and does not touch any Flow State application code. The available TUs (tu_ui, tu_scene, tu_sim, tu_sketch, tu_input) have no domain expertise relevant to CLI tooling changes. Per the Quality Unit Handbook: "Template/process changes may need no TUs." QA review is sufficient for this change.

RE-REVIEW: CR-103 v0.1 (revision cycle 2)

Previous deficiency has been corrected. Re-verification of all review criteria:

1. Frontmatter title: PASS. Value: "Establish docs/ and manual/ directories in qms-cli"
2. Frontmatter revision_summary: PASS. Value: "Fix file count (6 → 7) per QA review" — accurate and descriptive of the revision.
3. Document structure: PASS. All 12 required CR sections present per template.
4. Placeholder content: PASS. No {{...}}, TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found. Execution placeholders [...] are appropriately confined to Sections 10-11.
5. Traceability: PASS. INV-013 (CLOSED), CR-102 (CLOSED), SDLC-QMS-RS (EFFECTIVE v19.0), SDLC-QMS-RTM (EFFECTIVE v24.0) all confirmed. Commit 6e73be8 in Section 7.5 matches current qms-cli HEAD.
6. Internal consistency: PASS. File count now consistently reads "7" across all six references (Sections 2.2, 2.3, 4, 5.1, 7.1, 9.3). EI table maps 1:1 to implementation phases.
7. VR flags: PASS. Only EI-9 (integration verification) flagged — appropriate for behavioral verification.
8. Scope clarity: PASS. Explicit deferral of pipe-dream/QMS-Docs/ migration stated.
9. Impact assessment: PASS. Files, documents, existing projects, new projects, and development controls addressed. Qualified state continuity table verified against actual system state.
10. Formatting: PASS. Consistent headings, rules, bold, code blocks, and tables throughout.
11. Typos/errors: PASS. None found.
12. No unintended changes: PASS. Only lines 3, 24, and 82 differ from previous version — all are the expected corrections.

DECISION: RECOMMEND. All review criteria satisfied. No deficiencies found. Document is ready for approval routing.

POST-REVIEW: CR-103 v1.1 — NON-COMPLIANT

I read the complete draft (lines 1-325) and verified against the post-review checklist for Code CRs. The document has three deficiencies that must be corrected.

--- VERIFIED (with evidence) ---

1. Frontmatter title: "Establish docs/ and manual/ directories in qms-cli" — present and non-empty.

2. Frontmatter revision_summary: "Fix file count (6 -> 7) per QA review" — present and describes v1.0-to-v1.1 change.

3. SDLC Prerequisites:
   - SDLC-QMS-RS v20.0: EFFECTIVE (confirmed via qms_status)
   - SDLC-QMS-RTM v25.0: EFFECTIVE (confirmed via qms_status)
   - Both are in force. Prerequisite met.

4. Merge complete: EI-11 records "PR #20 merged to main (54708d2), qms-cli submodule updated" — merge commit recorded.

5. EI execution summaries verified (EI-1 through EI-11):
   - EI-1: Commit 91cba86 (baseline) — traceable artifact
   - EI-2: .test-env/qms-cli setup, branch cr-103/exec, 677 baseline tests — adequate
   - EI-3: SDLC-QMS-RS v20.0 EFFECTIVE — independently confirmed
   - EI-4: Commit ea8f1e2, 7 files in docs/ — traceable
   - EI-5: Commit 10c55df, git mv seed/docs/ to manual/ — traceable
   - EI-6: Commit 3ee36e3, init.py and seed/claude.md changes — traceable
   - EI-7: Commit f6717a0, 678 tests pass — traceable, matches RTM qualified commit
   - EI-8: CI run 22330012415, qualified commit f6717a0 — traceable
   - EI-9: Integration verification, QMS-Docs/ not created — see Deficiency 2
   - EI-10: SDLC-QMS-RTM v25.0 EFFECTIVE — independently confirmed
   - EI-11: PR #20 merged (54708d2) — traceable

6. Qualified commit traceability: f6717a0 is the qualified commit in RTM v25.0. PR #20 merged to main at 54708d2. The qualified commit must be an ancestor of the merge commit for the verified code to be in production.

7. Scope integrity: 12 EIs cover the full scope described in Sections 2 and 9. Implementation phases map 1:1 to the implementation plan. No scope items appear to have been dropped.

8. Execution Comments (line 302): Documents the QA correction cycle — "QA flagged RS v19.0 reference in RTM Purpose section during review — corrected before approval." This matches the review record from my RTM review earlier this session.

9. No child VARs or ERs expected (no failed EIs except see Deficiency 3).

--- DEFICIENCIES ---

DEFICIENCY 1: Placeholder content in Section 11 (Execution Summary).

Line 308 reads: "[EXECUTION_SUMMARY]"

This is placeholder content. Section 11 must contain an actual execution summary describing the overall outcome of execution, not a placeholder tag. Per review criteria, placeholder content is grounds for rejection.

DEFICIENCY 2: VR-flagged EI without a corresponding VR document.

EI-9 (line 291) has "Yes" in the VR column, indicating a Verification Record is required. However, no VR child document exists for CR-103 (confirmed by searching QMS/CR/CR-103/ — only CR-103-draft.md is present, with no VR files).

Per the Quality Unit Handbook: "VR exists for every VR-flagged EI. Check the VR column — every 'Yes' (or VR ID) must have a corresponding completed VR."

Either create and complete the VR, or if the integration verification was adequately evidenced through other means and a VR is not actually warranted, remove the "Yes" from the VR column and provide the evidence directly in the EI execution summary.

DEFICIENCY 3: EI-12 outcome is "Pending" — not a valid outcome.

Line 294 reads: "Pending — final commit with all QMS artifacts | Pending"

Per the Quality Unit Handbook: "Every EI has an outcome. No blanks. Every row is Pass or Fail." The value "Pending" is neither Pass nor Fail.

I understand the post-execution commit cannot be completed until after post-approval. If EI-12 is not yet executable at this stage, document why in the execution summary and clarify the expected completion timing. However, the outcome column must contain a valid value or the EI must be clearly marked as not yet due with procedural justification.

--- DISPOSITION ---

Request updates. Three deficiencies require correction before post-review can recommend.
